nodes.label: Personalized daily content digest
nodes.description: Deliver a concise daily digest of relevant content tailored to a user profile.

nodes.label: Unattended overnight execution
nodes.description: Run the full pipeline on a schedule without manual intervention and recover from failures.

nodes.label: Multi-source public content ingestion
nodes.description: Collect content from blogs, news, RSS feeds, and forums, including pagination and basic dynamic loading.

nodes.label: Persistent cross-run deduplication
nodes.description: Store content in a persistent database and prevent duplicate records across runs.

nodes.label: Profile-based filtering and ranking
nodes.description: Score and rank items by topics, keywords, and preferred sources from a user profile.

nodes.label: Summaries and formatted digest output
nodes.description: Generate concise summaries for relevant items and produce a clean daily digest in email or PDF form.

nodes.label: Robust logging and error recovery
nodes.description: Provide structured logging, fault handling, and retry/recovery behavior for unattended runs.

nodes.label: Snapshot-based parser validation
nodes.description: Validate parsing and extraction logic using saved snapshots instead of live source calls.

nodes.label: Use Python as orchestration runtime
nodes.description: Implement ingestion, ranking, summarization orchestration, and scheduling in Python.
nodes.rationale: Strong ecosystem for scraping, NLP, data processing, and testing with low operational overhead for a single-project pipeline.

nodes.label: Use PostgreSQL for persistent content store
nodes.description: Store normalized content, fingerprints, user profiles, runs, and digest artifacts in PostgreSQL.
nodes.rationale: Better concurrency, indexing, and reliability for long-running growth than SQLite, while keeping schema flexibility.

nodes.label: Hybrid feed-first collection strategy
nodes.description: Use RSS/API ingestion first and only use headless browser scraping for sources that require dynamic rendering.
nodes.rationale: Reduces fragility, runtime, and bot-detection risk compared with full browser scraping everywhere.

nodes.label: Target sources permit automated collection
nodes.description: Assumes robots policies and terms permit the planned level of ingestion and scraping.

nodes.label: User profile provides enough relevance signal
nodes.description: Assumes topics, keywords, and source preferences are sufficient to rank content accurately.

nodes.label: LLM summarization cost and latency are acceptable
nodes.description: Assumes nightly volume can be summarized within budget and overnight runtime constraints.

nodes.label: Source layout or markup drift
nodes.description: Site template changes can silently break extraction logic and reduce digest quality.

nodes.label: Bot mitigation blocks collection
nodes.description: Anti-bot controls may block dynamic scraping paths and reduce source coverage.

nodes.label: Digest quality misses user intent
nodes.description: Filtering and summarization may over-compress or miss relevant items, reducing trust.
